ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-09-2010 IN DEPT. 220, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE HELD. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(B). THE TRIAL IS MARCH 28, 2011 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 220. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS MARCH 17. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT TO BE FILED ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. PLAINTIFFS TO MAKE A DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS BY MARCH 10. MSC ON MARCH 17 AT 1 PM IN ROOM 603. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING ANY OTHER SCHEDULING OR CASE MANAGEMENT ISSUES THAT ANYONE WISHES TO HAVE ADDRESSED IN THE PREFERENCE ORDER. PLAINTIFF SARAH BLAZIEK, AS THE DAUGHTER OF THE DECEDENT, HAS A SUBSTANTIAL INTEREST IN THE CASE. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 30 DAYS AFTER THE GRANTING OF PREFERENCE. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL RELY ON PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 50 DAYS AFTER THE GRANTING OF PREFERENCE. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. EXPERT DISCOVERY SHALL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL SWEARING IN OF THE JURY AND ALL OTHER DISCOVERY REMAIN OPEN UNTIL THE TRIAL DATE OF 3/28/11. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: E. BURA; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)
